title: STERNBERG DIFFICULT
description: This dataset contains raw EEG recordings from participants performing a modified Sternberg working memory task with varying set sizes (3, 6, 9, 12, and 15 Russian consonant letters). The study investigates neural correlates of working memory load and cognitive difficulty, with participants' Raven Progressive Standard Matrices scores provided for cognitive ability assessment. The dataset includes 200 trials per participant organized in difficulty blocks with standardized encoding, retention, and retrieval phases.
methods_description: EEG data were recorded during a Sternberg task where participants viewed sets of Russian consonant letters (set sizes: 3, 6, 9, 12, 15) followed by probe letter recognition. Each trial consisted of a 500-1000 ms fixation cross, 1500 ms encoding phase, 2000 ms retention phase, and 1500 ms retrieval phase. Participants responded via button press (left arrow for 'no', right arrow for 'yes'). Stimuli were presented using PsychoPy2. Participants completed 200 trials total (40 trials per set size block) with breaks between blocks.
